scope.summary:
Goal: turn editor context into FEMS (Pillar 12 typed-memory system, Pillar 12) memory-write PROPOSALS that are always review-gated and NEVER directly committed by the editor. Add a 'Propose to Memory' command that operates on the current SharedSelection (any editor pane: code, rich-text, loom block, canvas). When invoked, the command builds a typed memory-write proposal carrying full source provenance, submits it to the EXISTING review-gated FEMS write path (the proposal endpoint), and emits an FR-EVT-MEM-001 (memory_write_proposed) Flight Recorder event through the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter. The commit step stays operator/review-gated downstream; this MT only creates the PROPOSAL and the observability event. This is the editor entry point into the FEMS proposal->review->commit loop.

This MT is PURE FRONTEND. No backend build, no schema changes, no SQLite anywhere. It binds the EXISTING handshake_core FEMS proposal API. If the proposal endpoint does not exist on the current API surface, STOP and raise a TYPED BLOCKER (do not invent, add, or rewrite a backend endpoint; do not fall back to a direct memory commit).

File to CREATE:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/memory_proposal.rs (~300-450 lines) — defines the proposal data model, the pure proposal-builder function, the typed blocker, and the egui command/dialog wiring. Lives under a new (or existing) src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/ module so all Pillar-12 editor integration is colocated. If src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/mod.rs does not yet exist, create it and add `pub mod memory_proposal;`; otherwise edit the existing src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/mod.rs to add the module declaration. Also register the module in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/lib.rs (`pub mod fems;`) if fems is not already declared there.

File to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs — register the 'Propose to Memory' command into the WP-011 command registry / command bus (command_registry.rs) so it appears in the command palette and can be keybound. The command reads the live SharedSelection from the WP-011 selection/event substrate (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), builds the proposal, and dispatches submission. Do NOT duplicate the registry/bus; reuse the existing command_registry.rs registration API and the SharedSelection that the WP-011 command/event substrate already owns. Wire the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter (already wired through event_bus.rs per MT-036) to emit the FR event after a successful proposal submission.

Proposal data model (in memory_proposal.rs):
  pub enum MemoryClass { Episodic, Semantic, Procedural }
  pub struct MemorySourceProvenance { document_id: String, selection_start: usize, selection_end: usize, content_hash: String, pane_id: String, workspace_id: String }
  pub struct MemoryWriteProposal { class: MemoryClass, content: String, source: MemorySourceProvenance, review_gated: bool, actor_id: String }
  pub enum MemoryProposalError { MissingEndpoint, NoSelection, SubmitFailed(String) }  // MissingEndpoint == the TYPED BLOCKER

Pure proposal-builder (trivially unit-testable, no I/O):
  pub fn build_proposal(sel: &SharedSelection, class: MemoryClass, workspace_id: &str, actor_id: &str) -> Result<MemoryWriteProposal, MemoryProposalError>
  - Reads document_id, selection start/end byte offsets, pane_id, and the selected text from the SharedSelection variant (TextRange { pane_id, start, end, text } per MT-031; BlockRef / NodeRef variants resolve their document_id and use a whole-block/whole-node range). Returns MemoryProposalError::NoSelection if SharedSelection is None.
  - content_hash: compute a stable SHA-256 (or the same hashing primitive MT-032 / the loom content_hash uses — reuse the loom_graph.rs content-hash primitive, do not introduce a second hashing scheme) over the exact selected content; store as lowercase hex in MemorySourceProvenance.content_hash.
  - review_gated: ALWAYS true for Procedural class (spec requires procedural-class proposals to be review-gated); for Episodic/Semantic default to true as well (commit is never editor-direct). Never set review_gated=false from the editor.

Submission (off the egui frame thread, same pattern as MT-036 emit):
  pub async fn submit_proposal(proposal: &MemoryWriteProposal, client: &HandshakeCoreClient) -> Result<ProposalAck, MemoryProposalError>
  - POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals with the typed proposal body (existing review-gated FEMS write path). On 404 / route-absent / capability-missing, return MemoryProposalError::MissingEndpoint — this is the TYPED BLOCKER the coder surfaces; do NOT silently commit or fall back to any direct memory-write route.
  - On success, parse ProposalAck { proposal_id, status } and trigger the FR event emit.

FR event (reuse MT-036 emitter; do NOT build a new emitter):
  - After a successful submit, emit a NativeEditorEvent through the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter (wired via event_bus.rs) whose action maps to FR-EVT-MEM-001 (memory_write_proposed). The payload carries: { action: 'memory_write_proposed', proposal_id, class, document_id, selection_start, selection_end, content_hash, review_gated, pane_id }. The event_type stays 'system' and schema stays the closed FR schema per MT-036 (do NOT invent a new event_type; embed the native_editor payload as MT-036 specifies). FR-EVT-MEM-001 is the stable event-name marker carried in the payload action, not a new ledger schema.

egui command / dialog wiring:
  - Register CommandDescriptor { id: 'fems.propose_to_memory', label: 'Propose to Memory', keybind: None (palette-driven; do not steal a VS Code binding), handler } into the WP-011 command registry via command_registry.rs (the same registration API that backs command_palette.rs).
  - The handler reads the live SharedSelection from the WP-011 command/event substrate (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), opens a small egui confirmation dialog letting the operator pick MemoryClass (Episodic/Semantic/Procedural radio) and preview the selected content + computed content_hash, then on confirm fires submit_proposal off-thread.
  - AccessKit: command palette entry already covered by MT-031 (author_id 'cmd-fems.propose_to_memory' via the cmd-{id} convention). The dialog gets author_id 'fems-propose-dialog' role Dialog; the class radios get author_id 'fems-class-{episodic|semantic|procedural}' role RadioButton; the confirm button gets author_id 'fems-propose-confirm' role Button. Register all ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs), never ad-hoc.

Reuse WP-011 shell primitives: command registry / command palette (command_registry.rs + command_palette.rs — register the command), the WP-011 command/event substrate that owns SharedSelection (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), and the AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs for the dialog + radio + button ids). Reuse the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter (wired via event_bus.rs) for the FR event. Reuse the MT-037 knowledge_documents client wiring / HandshakeCoreClient (built on backend_client.rs) for the HTTP call. Do not duplicate any of those substrates.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Create src/frontend/handshake_native/src/fems/memory_proposal.rs with the data model, build_proposal (pure), submit_proposal (async), and the dialog/command wiring.
  2. Make build_proposal pure and side-effect-free so it is trivially unit-testable; submission and FR emit are separate steps that consume its output.
  3. content_hash MUST reuse the loom/MT-032 content-hash primitive in loom_graph.rs (same algorithm, same hex encoding) so a proposal's hash matches the document's block hash for the same content; do not introduce a second hashing scheme.
  4. review_gated is hard-set true for Procedural and never settable to false from the editor; assert this in a unit test.
  5. submit_proposal runs off the egui frame thread (tokio::spawn or the same async mechanism MT-036 uses); never block the frame.
  6. On MissingEndpoint, surface the typed blocker to the coder/validator (return the typed error, log to the MT-036 in-memory error ring, and do NOT commit anything). The absence of the proposal endpoint is a BLOCKER, not a reason to write memory directly.
  7. After a successful ack, emit FR-EVT-MEM-001 via the MT-036 emitter; if the emit fails it must not crash the frame (MT-036 error-ring behavior).
  8. Tests: (a) unit build_proposal sets class + full source provenance (document_id, selection_start/end, content_hash, pane_id) correctly from a TextRange selection; (b) unit Procedural-class proposal has review_gated=true and is never auto-committed (no direct-commit code path exists); (c) integration (real PG): invoking 'Propose to Memory' on a selection creates a proposal record via the FEMS proposal endpoint AND an FR-EVT-MEM-001 (memory_write_proposed) event appears in the flight-recorder ledger; (d) unit missing-endpoint path returns MemoryProposalError::MissingEndpoint (the typed blocker) and performs no commit and no silent fallback.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - WP-011 command/event substrate (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs, owning the command bus + SharedSelection) MUST be present — the command registers into command_registry.rs and reads SharedSelection from the WP-011 substrate. This is a cross-WP dependency on the WP-011 native shell, not an intra-WP MT dependency.
  - MT-031 (CommandBus + SharedSelection editor binding) MUST be merged — the command registers into the command registry and reads SharedSelection. (MT-031 is delivered against the WP-011 substrate; see implementation_notes for the cross-WP relationship.)
  - MT-036 (NativeEditorEventEmitter + FR runtime_chat_event wiring through event_bus.rs) MUST be merged — the FR-EVT-MEM-001 emit reuses this emitter; do not build a new one.
  - MT-037 (knowledge documents API binding / HandshakeCoreClient on backend_client.rs) MUST be merged — provides the typed HTTP client used for the proposal POST.
  - MT-063 MUST be merged — provides the preceding FEMS editor-integration surface this MT builds on.
  - The FEMS proposal endpoint (POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals) must exist on the current handshake_core API surface; if absent, raise the typed blocker.

Risk if missed: Editing work cannot feed the memory pillar; FEMS loses its richest provenance source (operator/model selections in real documents with exact document_id + range + content hash) and the proposal->review->commit loop has no editor entry point. Without it, Pillar 12 memory is fed only by non-editor sources and the native editors fail the FEMS interop edge.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: build_proposal is a pure function over SharedSelection and sets MemoryClass plus full MemorySourceProvenance (document_id, selection_start, selection_end, content_hash, pane_id, workspace_id) correctly — proven by cargo test build_proposal over a TextRange selection asserting every provenance field matches the selection.

AC-002: A Procedural-class proposal has review_gated=true and there is NO code path that auto-commits it; review_gated is never settable to false from the editor — proven by cargo test asserting review_gated==true for Procedural and that no direct memory-commit call site exists (grep gate: no call to any commit/write-direct route from memory_proposal.rs).

AC-003: content_hash reuses the loom/MT-032 content-hash primitive in loom_graph.rs (same algorithm + lowercase-hex encoding); no second hashing scheme is introduced — proven by grep showing the import of the shared loom_graph.rs hashing helper and a unit test asserting the hash of a known string equals the loom block hash for the same content.

AC-004: Invoking 'Propose to Memory' on a live selection creates a proposal record via POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals AND emits an FR-EVT-MEM-001 (memory_write_proposed) event that appears in GET /api/flight_recorder — proven by integration test against real PG.

AC-005: When the FEMS proposal endpoint is absent, submit_proposal returns MemoryProposalError::MissingEndpoint (the typed blocker), performs no commit, and does no silent direct-memory fallback — proven by unit test simulating a 404/route-absent response.

AC-006: The 'fems.propose_to_memory' command is registered into the WP-011 command registry (command_registry.rs; appears in command_palette.rs) and reads SharedSelection from the WP-011 command/event substrate (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs); no new command channel or duplicate registry/bus is created — proven by grep of the command_registry.rs registration call and absence of a second command registry.

AC-007: AccessKit nodes 'fems-propose-dialog' (Dialog), 'fems-class-procedural' (RadioButton), and 'fems-propose-confirm' (Button) are present with correct roles and registered through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) — proven by kittest AccessKit tree dump.

AC-008: The FR event reuses the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter (no new emitter), keeps event_type='system' and the closed FR schema, and carries action='memory_write_proposed' with proposal_id, class, document_id, selection range, content_hash, and review_gated in the payload — proven by unit test on the emitted payload shape.
AC-009: No src/backend/** files are modified, no new endpoints are added, and no SQLite dependency is introduced — proven by diff gate.
AC-010: cargo test -p handshake-native memory_proposal passes with no failures and no panics.
proof_targets: PT-001: cargo test -p handshake-native build_proposal -- --nocapture  =>  GREEN; asserts class + full source provenance set from a TextRange selection.
PT-002: cargo test -p handshake-native procedural_review_gated -- --nocapture  =>  GREEN; asserts review_gated==true for Procedural and no auto-commit path.

PT-003: integration test (real PG): invoke 'Propose to Memory' on a selection, assert a proposal record exists via the FEMS proposal endpoint AND an FR-EVT-MEM-001 (memory_write_proposed) event appears in GET /api/flight_recorder.
PT-004: cargo test -p handshake-native missing_endpoint_blocker -- --nocapture  =>  GREEN; simulated route-absent response returns MemoryProposalError::MissingEndpoint, no commit, no silent fallback.
PT-005: kittest AccessKit tree dump: assert 'fems-propose-dialog' (Dialog), 'fems-class-procedural' (RadioButton), 'fems-propose-confirm' (Button) present with correct roles.

PT-006: grep proof: command_registry.rs registration for 'fems.propose_to_memory' present; loom_graph.rs content-hash helper imported (no duplicate hasher); MT-036 emitter reused (no new emitter); no src/backend/** change; no SQLite dependency.
implementation_notes: Cross-WP dependency (prose, not depends_on): this MT consumes the WP-011 native shell substrate — command_registry.rs + command_palette.rs (command bus + palette), event_bus.rs (event substrate that carries the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter), accessibility/registry.rs (AccessKit id registry), loom_graph.rs (loom content-hash primitive), and backend_client.rs (HTTP client under HandshakeCoreClient). These WP-011 modules must be present; they are not WP-012 microtasks and so are tracked here as prose, not in depends_on.
Keep build_proposal pure and side-effect-free (no I/O, no async) so it is trivially unit-testable; submission and FR emit consume its output as separate steps.

content_hash MUST reuse the loom/MT-032 content-hash primitive in loom_graph.rs (same algorithm, same lowercase-hex encoding) so a proposal hash equals the document block hash for identical content; importing a second hashing crate is a defect.
review_gated is a hard invariant: always true for Procedural per spec, and never settable to false from the editor for any class; the commit step is downstream and review-gated, never editor-direct.
submit_proposal must run off the egui frame thread (tokio::spawn or the MT-036 async mechanism); on failure log to the MT-036 in-memory error ring, never block or crash the frame.

The TYPED BLOCKER is MemoryProposalError::MissingEndpoint: if POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals is absent from the handshake_core surface, surface the blocker and do NOT write memory directly. Confirm the endpoint exists in src/backend/handshake_core (read-only inspection) before assuming absence.

Reuse the MT-031 SharedSelection variants: TextRange { pane_id, start, end, text } gives document_id (via pane->document mapping), exact byte range, and selected text; BlockRef / NodeRef resolve their document_id and use a whole-block/whole-node range. SharedSelection is owned by the WP-011 command/event substrate (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs).

Reuse the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter (wired through event_bus.rs) and its closed FR schema; the FR-EVT-MEM-001 name is carried in the payload action ('memory_write_proposed'), not as a new ledger event_type.

Register the command (command_registry.rs), and the dialog/radios/confirm-button ids (accessibility/registry.rs) through the WP-011 registries so swarm agents can drive the proposal flow deterministically.
MemoryClass radio default: Episodic (the most common editor->memory case); the operator can switch to Semantic or Procedural in the dialog before confirming.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/* Front End Memory Panel (write-review/proposal reference) — the React memory panel's proposal/write-review UI: how a proposal is composed, the class selection, the provenance display, and the review-gated (never direct-commit) submission pattern; port the data model and the review-gate semantics, not the React rendering.
binds_backend_api: POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/memory/proposals — existing review-gated FEMS write path; submit a typed MemoryWriteProposal (class, content, source provenance, review_gated, actor_id). REUSE ONLY. If absent from the current handshake_core surface, raise MemoryProposalError::MissingEndpoint as a TYPED BLOCKER — do not add, rewrite, or bypass with a direct memory-commit route. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only; no SQLite anywhere.

POST /api/flight_recorder/runtime_chat_event — FR-EVT-MEM-001 (memory_write_proposed) emitted via the MT-036 NativeEditorEventEmitter (no new emitter). Body is the closed RuntimeChatEventV0_1 schema per MT-036; the native_editor payload carries action='memory_write_proposed' with proposal_id, class, document_id, selection range, content_hash, review_gated, pane_id. REUSE ONLY.

risks: RISK-001: The editor commits memory directly (or a fallback path writes memory) instead of creating a review-gated proposal — violating the never-editor-direct invariant and bypassing FEMS review.
RISK-002: A Procedural-class proposal is auto-committed or has review_gated set false, violating the spec requirement that procedural-class proposals are review-gated.

RISK-003: Source provenance is incomplete or wrong (missing document_id, wrong selection range, or a content_hash computed with a different algorithm than the loom block hash), so the proposal cannot be traced back to its exact origin and dedup/verification against the source document breaks.
RISK-004: The FEMS proposal endpoint is absent and the code silently swallows the failure or invents a direct-write fallback instead of raising the typed MissingEndpoint blocker.
RISK-005: A second hashing scheme is introduced for content_hash, so proposal hashes never match document block hashes and provenance verification false-negatives.
RISK-006: submit_proposal runs on the egui frame thread or unbounded task spawning floods the backend on repeated 'Propose to Memory' invocations, freezing or destabilizing the UI.

RISK-007: The FR-EVT-MEM-001 event is not emitted, is emitted with a wrong/invented event_type, or omits required provenance fields, breaking swarm observability of the memory-proposal loop (HBR-SWARM).
RISK-008: A new command channel or duplicate command registry/bus is created instead of reusing the WP-011 command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs substrate, fragmenting the command/interaction invariant.

RISK-009: Scope creep into src/backend/** (adding/rewriting the proposal endpoint) or introducing SQLite for a local proposal cache, violating the no-backend-rewrite and PostgreSQL/EventLedger-only constraints.
RISK-010: The dialog steals a VS Code keybinding or AccessKit ids collide/are ad-hoc, breaking parity and deterministic swarm addressing.
minimum_controls: MC-001: A grep/diff gate asserts memory_proposal.rs contains NO direct memory-commit/write call site — only the proposal POST; the only write path is the review-gated proposal endpoint.
MC-002: A unit test asserts review_gated==true for Procedural class and that no constructor or method can set review_gated=false from the editor.

MC-003: A unit test asserts build_proposal populates every MemorySourceProvenance field (document_id, selection_start, selection_end, content_hash, pane_id, workspace_id) from the selection; a separate test asserts content_hash equals the loom/MT-032 hash (loom_graph.rs) for the same content.
MC-004: A unit test simulating a route-absent (404) response asserts MemoryProposalError::MissingEndpoint is returned, no commit occurs, and no fallback write is attempted.
MC-005: A grep gate asserts the content-hash helper is imported from the shared loom_graph.rs (MT-032) module and no second hashing dependency is added.

MC-006: submit_proposal runs off the frame thread; bound concurrency with the MT-036 semaphore pattern (drop + error-ring on saturation); a test asserts show()/handler never performs blocking network I/O on the frame thread.

MC-007: A unit test asserts the emitted FR event reuses the MT-036 emitter, keeps event_type='system', carries action='memory_write_proposed', and includes proposal_id, class, document_id, selection range, content_hash, and review_gated.
MC-008: A grep gate asserts the command is registered via the WP-011 command_registry.rs registration API for 'fems.propose_to_memory' and no second command registry/bus is introduced.
MC-009: A diff gate confirms no src/backend/** changes, no new endpoints, and no SQLite dependency added; backend access is the two bound POST APIs only.

MC-010: AccessKit ids ('fems-propose-dialog', 'fems-class-{episodic|semantic|procedural}', 'fems-propose-confirm') are registered through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) and de-duplicated; the command uses no VS Code-reserved keybinding (palette-driven, keybind=None).
